subject: Computer Networking
title: DNS: The Internet's Phone Book
description: A 60-second lesson on DNS: The Internet's Phone Book.

hook.text: You remember 'amazon.com' but not 205.251.242.103. DNS bridges that gap for you automatically.

buildup.text: DNS servers store mappings from domain names to IP addresses. Your device queries them constantly.

discovery.text: It's hierarchical: root servers point to .com servers, which point to amazon.com's name server.

twist.text: DNS answers are cached locally, so repeat lookups are instant. But stale caches can serve old addresses.

climax.text: If DNS goes down, the internet still works — but nobody can find anything by name. Effectively broken.

punchline.text: DNS turns names into numbers. Without it, you'd memorize IPs.

quiz.question: What does DNS do?
options: Translates domain names into IP addresses
